# Story 8 Final — Reorganize Workers / Runs / Sessions / Executions tabs

## Diagnosis (unchanged from /tmp/story-8-plan.md)

Current project-level tabs: Workers, Runs, Sessions, Executions. Sessions and Executions duplicate concepts already representable in the three-layer Run model (`layer=run`, `layer=try`) but are NOT yet equivalent in storage:

- `AgentSession` carries fields the `Run` type does not: `billingMode`, `tokens.cached`, `prompt`, `response`, `stderr`, `outcome`, `detail`, `baseRev`, `resultRev`. (`schema.graphql:650`, `:1091`)
- Code currently maps execution bundles to `RunLayerRun`, not `RunLayerTry` as my first plan assumed (`state_runs.go:164`).
- Some `AgentSession` rows have no associated `Run` (raw `ddx agent log` invocations, `beadId` nullable).

So Runs-as-shell can't fully replace Sessions/Executions until a data canonicalization step lands.

## Proposed New Structure (revised per codex)

Two project-level activity tabs:

| Tab | Contents |
|---|---|
| **Workers** | Live + recent worker processes for this repo (project-scoped). Unchanged structure. |
| **Runs** | Unified history shell (work / try / run layers). `AgentSession` survives as the **transcript/cost detail backing** for `layer=run` rows; `.ddx/executions/` bundles are the **detail backing** for `layer=try` rows. |

Sessions and Executions are retired as top-level nav. They survive as:
- Layer chip on Runs (`layer=run` for sessions, `layer=try` for execution bundles).
- Per-row inline expansion that pulls full session/bundle detail.
- Existing detail URLs preserved for one deprecation window via 302/307 redirects (NOT 301 — codex finding 6).

Canonical mapping (corrected per codex finding 2): `.ddx/executions/` execute-bead bundles are **try-layer attempts**; the linked `AgentSession` is the child **run-layer invocation**. Existing `state_runs.go` synthesis must be corrected before UI consolidation, OR the temporary mismatch must be explicitly documented and the UI built layer-agnostic until the backing is fixed.

### Re-queue (revised)
- Mutation `runRequeue(input: { runId, params, idempotencyKey }): Run` — `idempotencyKey` (or `clientMutationId`) is **mandatory**.
- Server stores an idempotency record keyed by `(projectId, sourceRunId, idempotencyKey)` AND, for try-layer, atomically claims a `(projectId, beadId, active-try)` uniqueness record. Re-checks bead status at mutation time, not dialog-open time.
- **try-layer**: re-queue requires open bead + no active try. Confirms params, allows `from <rev>` override.
- **run-layer**: re-queue requires replayable prompt/harness/model + idempotencyKey; no bead-state precondition unless `beadId` is set.
- **work-layer**: NEW — "Start worker from this drain" action (codex finding 5). Prefills queue inputs, stop condition, selected beads, harness/profile from the original work run. Different label from "Re-queue" but same dispatch path.
- Closed bead re-queue blocked with "Reopen bead first" link.
- Successful run re-queue requires double-confirmation.

### Workers repo-scoping
Already correct in route. Audit `subscribeWorkerProgress` callers + add e2e proving cross-project workers do not appear.

## Spec changes
- **FEAT-008**: replace tab inventory; document Workers + Runs only; document layer-filter pattern; document re-queue UX; document URL deprecation policy (302 with sunset header, parallel routes for one release).
- **FEAT-010**: declare `Run` is the canonical historical record, but `AgentSession` and `.ddx/executions/` bundles remain lossless detail backings; document the bundle-as-try-layer correction.
- **FEAT-021**: Workers tile = project-scoped running workers; Runs tile = last-N runs across layers.
- **FEAT-019**: re-queue is the manual counterpart to evaluation auto-retry; same backend mutation.

## Implementation surface
- **Backend**:
  - `Mutation.runRequeue` with mandatory idempotencyKey, atomic claim record.
  - Correct `state_runs.go` bundle layering OR document the deferred mismatch.
  - Resolver join exposing AgentSession detail fields when expanding a `layer=run` Run row.
- **Frontend**:
  - Merge Sessions and Executions detail panels into Runs row expansion (layer-aware).
  - Add search input, multi-column sort, reuse Story 6 chip/sort/search **primitives** (not the full page model — codex finding 7); reuse Story 7 virtualization infra.
  - Add Re-queue button + confirmation dialog (try, run); add "Start worker from this drain" for work.
  - Add 302/307 redirects: `/sessions` → `/runs?layer=run`, `/sessions#<id>` → `/runs?layer=run&session=<id>`, `/executions[?bead=&verdict=&harness=&q=&after=]` → `/runs?layer=try` with query mapping preserved, `/executions/[id]` → `/runs/[runId]?bundle=true`.
  - Sweep internal links in `shellRoutes.ts`, `BeadDetail.svelte`, `commits/+page.svelte`, `agent/+page.ts`, Workers detail (codex finding 6).
  - Drop Sessions/Executions from `NavShell.svelte` AFTER redirects + link sweep land.
- **Tests**:
  - e2e: nav shows Workers + Runs only; old URLs redirect with query preserved.
  - e2e: every legacy `agentSessions(projectId)` row appears in Runs (lossless migration AC, codex finding 3).
  - e2e: layer-aware row expansion shows session detail (run), bundle detail (try), queue inputs (work).
  - e2e: workers project-scoped; cross-project worker not visible.
  - e2e: re-queue happy path; idempotency under double-click; closed-bead block; successful-run double-confirm.
  - e2e: work-layer "Start worker from this drain" prefills inputs.
  - Unit: `runRequeue` resolver — idempotencyKey enforcement, atomic claim, raw-run dedupe.

## Acceptance criteria (revised)
1. Project nav lists exactly two activity tabs: **Workers**, **Runs**.
2. `/sessions`, `/executions`, `/executions/[id]` return 302/307 redirects to filtered `/runs` URLs with all query params preserved; sunset header set.
3. Workers tab lists only workers whose `projectId` matches the current project.
4. Runs tab supports: layer filter, status filter, harness filter, free-text search (bead id / title / output excerpt), column sort (Started / Duration / Cost).
5. Each Runs row expands inline to layer-appropriate detail: run-layer shows session prompt/response/stderr/billing/cached tokens (lossless port from Sessions); try-layer shows bundle metadata + check results + verdict (lossless port from Executions); work-layer shows queue inputs + child run links.
6. Re-queue button: visible on try-layer rows when bead is open and no active try; visible on run-layer rows when prompt/harness/model are replayable; hidden on work-layer (replaced by "Start worker from this drain").
7. Re-queue on a successful run requires explicit double-confirmation.
8. Re-queue on a closed bead is disabled with a "Reopen bead first" link.
9. `runRequeue` GraphQL mutation requires `idempotencyKey`; server enforces atomic dedupe on `(projectId, sourceRunId, idempotencyKey)` and `(projectId, beadId, active-try)`; second click within race window returns the same Run id, not a duplicate.
10. Existing Workers panel UX (drain count, start/stop, live phase subscription) preserved unchanged.
11. **All `agentSessions(projectId)` rows are visible in Runs**, either through a joined Run row or a synthesized `layer=run` row keyed by session id; no session disappears during migration.
12. Telemetry/audit events emitted for: re-queue attempt, blocked-duplicate, closed-bead block, raw rerun, work re-drain.

## Risks
- URL bookmarks (mitigated by 302 + query preservation + sunset header).
- Re-queue duplicates (mitigated by mandatory idempotencyKey + atomic claim).
- Sessions/Run merge data loss (mitigated by Bead 8.0 canonicalization + AC11).
- Workers repo-scope leak via subscribeWorkerProgress (mitigated by audit bead + e2e).
- Test surface explosion (mitigated by porting existing e2e specs from sessions/ + executions/ before deletion).
- Bundle layer mismatch in `state_runs.go` (must be addressed by 8.0).

## Reuse from Story 6/7
- **Story 6** (search/sort/filter): reuse the chip / sort header / search input PRIMITIVES on Runs. Do NOT inherit page model — Runs has live updates, parent/child hierarchy, layer expansion, re-queue affordances.
- **Story 7** (virtualization): reuse the virtualization wrapper component when Runs > 1k rows. Live updates require a Runs-specific adapter on top.
- **Story 5** (categorization): row-expansion pattern only; no taxonomy reuse.

## Bead breakdown (final)

| Bead | Title | Deps | Tier |
|---|---|---|---|
| 8.0 | **Canonicalize Runs data projection**: correct bundle layering in `state_runs.go`; map AgentSession losslessly into Run row detail (resolver join); prove every legacy session has a Runs entry. | — | smart |
| 8.1 | Add `runRequeue` GraphQL mutation with mandatory `idempotencyKey`, atomic claim records, and raw-run dedupe. | 8.0 | smart |
| 8.2 | Merge Sessions + Executions detail into layer-aware Runs row expansion. | 8.0 | cheap-then-review |
| 8.3 | Add re-queue UI (try, run) + "Start worker from this drain" UI (work) + confirmation dialogs. | 8.1, 8.2 | cheap |
| 8.4 | Add 302/307 redirects for `/sessions`, `/executions`, `/executions/[id]` with query preservation; sweep internal links across `shellRoutes.ts`, `BeadDetail.svelte`, commits page, agent page; drop tabs from NavShell. | 8.2 | cheap |
| 8.5 | Audit Workers project-scoping (`subscribeWorkerProgress` callers + resolver) + add e2e proving no cross-project leak. | — | cheap |
| 8.6 | Reuse Story 6 chip/search primitives + Story 7 virtualization on Runs (Runs-specific adapter for live updates). | 8.2; Stories 6 & 7 landed | cheap |
| 8.7 | Docs + telemetry: update FEAT-008/010/019/021 specs; emit audit events for requeue attempts (success, blocked-duplicate, closed-bead, raw rerun, work re-drain). | 8.1, 8.3 | cheap |

8 beads. Tier rationale: 8.0 and 8.1 require careful data + concurrency reasoning (smart); rest is mechanical UI/wiring (cheap).

264	     | `preserved` | orange |
265	     | `failed` | red |
266	
267	   - Clicking a worker card expands to show the `recent_phases` timeline from
268	     `GET /api/projects/:project/workers/:id`. The expanded view also shows
269	     elapsed time, harness/model identity, cumulative token counts, and a link
270	     to the full execution evidence bundle for completed attempts.
271	   - For in-flight workers, the card auto-refreshes using the `workerProgress`
272	     subscription. The UI updates the phase badge, elapsed time, and token count from
273	     each progress event without re-fetching the full worker record. When the terminal
274	     phase event arrives, the stream closes and the card switches to a static completed state.
275	
276	7. **Epic execution view**
277	   - Lists open epics separately from single-ticket ready work
278	   - Shows epic branch name, epic worktree path, active child bead, and merge
279	     gate status
280	   - Shows sequential child-bead history on the epic branch
281	   - Allows the user to inspect the final epic merge candidate and merge-gate
282	     results before the merge commit is created
283	
284	8. **Provider / Harness Dashboard**
285	
286	   A dedicated page — separate from the status dashboard, agent log monitor,
287	   and worker progress view — that gives operators a clear picture of
288	   configured providers and the routing signals DDx uses to make harness
289	   decisions.
290	
291	   **Provider list panel (left/top):**
292	   - One row per configured harness (claude, codex, opencode, gemini, …)
293	   - Columns: harness name, display name, availability badge (`available` |
294	     `unavailable` | `unknown`), auth state badge, quota/headroom pill
295	     (`ok` | `blocked` | `unknown`), cost class, freshness timestamp
296	   - Searchable by harness name, model, status, or cost class
297	   - Sortable by any column; default sort by availability, then name
298	   - Filter chips: availability status, auth state, quota state, signal source
299	   - All data fetched from `GET /api/providers` on load; auto-refreshes on a
300	     configurable interval (default 60 s)
301	
302	   **Provider detail panel (right/bottom, shown on row click):**
303	   - Full routing signal snapshot for the selected harness
304	   - Per-model quota/headroom with source attribution and `unknown` when no
305	     stable live source exists
306	   - Historical usage table: 7-day and 30-day windows for input/output/total
307	     tokens and cost; `unknown` rendered as `—` not `0`
308	   - Burn estimate row: daily token rate, subscription burn class, confidence
309	     label (`high` / `medium` / `low`), freshness timestamp
310	   - Performance signals: p50/p95 latency, success rate, sample count, window
311	   - Signal source provenance list: which sources contributed to this snapshot
312	     (e.g. `stats-cache`, `native-session-jsonl`, `ddx-metrics`, `none`)
313	   - Data fetched from `GET /api/providers/:harness` on panel open
314	
315	   **Unknown-state semantics:**
316	   - `unknown` values are rendered with an explicit `—` or "unknown" label
317	     and a tooltip explaining why (e.g., "no stable non-PTY quota source
318	     confirmed for Claude")
319	   - Fields from provider-native sources are labeled "provider-reported"
320	   - Fields derived by DDx from observed metrics are labeled "DDx-estimated"
321	   - Fields that are genuinely absent carry a `?` badge, not a synthesized `0`
322	
323	   **Relationship to other views:**
324	   - Provider dashboard is host+user global (not project-scoped); it is
325	     accessible from the top-level navigation bar alongside the project picker
326	   - The status dashboard worker cards link to worker detail and the agent log
327	     monitor — not to the provider dashboard
328	   - The agent log monitor session detail links to the provider dashboard for
329	     the harness used in that invocation (via harness name)
330	   - The provider dashboard does NOT embed worker progress, bead state, or
331	     execution history; those live in their own dedicated views
332	
333	   **Reporting and export:**
334	   - "Copy JSON" action on the detail panel copies the `GET /api/providers/:harness`
335	     response verbatim — useful for bug reports and operator audits
336	   - Time-window selector (7d / 30d / custom) scopes the historical usage table
337	
338	9. **Persona viewer**
339	   - Browse personas with descriptions and tags
340	   - See which roles each persona is bound to in project config
341	   - View full persona content rendered as markdown
342	
343	### Non-Functional
344	
345	- **Performance:** Initial page load <2s. API calls <200ms. Graph renders smoothly for 100+ nodes.
346	- **Deployment:** Single binary. No external runtime dependencies. No separate frontend server in production.
347	- **Development:** `bun run dev` proxies to Go server. Hot reload for frontend. `go run` for backend.
348	- **Responsive:** Usable on tablet/laptop screens. Not optimized for mobile.
349	
350	## User Stories
351	
352	### US-080: Developer Browses Document Library
353	**As a** developer onboarding to a project
354	**I want** to open a web UI and browse all available documents
355	**So that** I can quickly understand what documents exist and how they're organized
356	
357	**Acceptance Criteria:**
358	- Given ddx-server is running, when I open `http://localhost:PORT`, then I am redirected to `/nodes/:nodeId` and see a dashboard with document/artifact counts by category
359	- Given I click on "Personas", then I see a list of all persona documents with names and descriptions
360	- Given I click on a persona, then I see its full content rendered as markdown
361	- Given I press the browser Back button, then I return to the Personas list with my scroll position preserved
362	
363	**E2E Test:** `artifacts.spec.ts` — full workflow: land on dashboard → navigate to personas → open persona → verify rendered content → navigate back
364	
365	### US-081: Developer Views and Navigates Dependency Graph
366	**As a** developer checking document health
367	**I want** to see a visual graph of document dependencies I can interact with
368	**So that** I can identify stale documents, understand relationships, and navigate directly to any doc from the graph
369	
370	**Acceptance Criteria:**
371	- Given I open the graph view, then I see nodes and edges representing dependency relationships; the graph renders with correct dimensions on first paint (no collapsed/zero-size SVG)
372	- Given documents are stale, then stale nodes are colored amber; documents with missing dependencies are colored red; fresh documents are green
373	- Given I click a node, then I navigate to that document's artifact detail view without a full page reload
374	- Given I press Back after clicking a node, then I return to the graph with the same zoom level and pan position restored
375	- Given I pan or zoom the graph, then the viewport state persists across the Back/Forward navigation cycle
376	- Given I apply a staleness filter chip (fresh / stale / missing), then only matching nodes and their connecting edges remain visible
377	- Given I apply a type filter chip, then only nodes of that media type remain visible
378	- Given I am on the graph page, then a `View documents` link is visible that navigates to the artifact browser filtered to markdown
379	- Given I am on an artifact detail page, then a `View in Graph` link navigates to the graph with that node highlighted
380	- Given the graph is rendered in either light or dark theme, then doc graph edges and arrowheads meet WCAG AA non-text contrast (>=3:1 against the canvas background) in both themes
381	
382	**E2E Test:** `graph.spec.ts` — full workflow: open graph → verify non-zero render → pan and zoom → click stale node → verify amber color → click node → verify artifact detail → Back → verify same viewport → apply staleness filter → verify node count changes → follow `View documents` link
383	
384	### US-081b: Developer Views and Regenerates Multi-Media Artifacts
385	**As a** developer working with generated project artifacts
386	**I want** the artifact browser to list all artifacts by type and render each
387	  one by media type, exposing regeneration when provenance exists
388	**So that** diagrams, images, PDFs, and markdown docs are all inspectable and
389	  refreshable without leaving the UI
390	
391	**Acceptance Criteria:**
392	- Given I navigate to `/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/artifacts`, then I see
393	  a list of all project artifacts with category filter chips and a search bar
394	- Given I filter by category (e.g. "images"), then only artifacts of that
395	  media type are shown and the URL updates to reflect the filter
396	- Given an artifact has `media_type: text/markdown`, when I open it, then I see
397	  rendered markdown with syntax-highlighted code blocks
398	- Given an artifact has Mermaid-generated SVG metadata, Excalidraw JSON,
399	  `image/*`, or `application/pdf`, when I open it, then the viewer renders the
400	  corresponding preview and still exposes the raw sidecar metadata
401	- Given an artifact's media type is unknown or binary-only, then the viewer
402	  shows an explicit metadata-only fallback with an open/download affordance
403	- Given an artifact has `generated_by`, then I see the producing run id,
404	  generator/prompt summary, source-hash status, and a `Regenerate` action
405	- Given I click `Regenerate`, then the UI calls `artifactRegenerate`, shows the
406	  returned run id, and provides a link to both the layer-aware run detail and
407	  the updated artifact — I can follow either link without losing my place
408	- Given `generated_by.source_hash` no longer matches, then only the generated
409	  artifact is marked stale; downstream `depends_on` staleness does not cascade
410	  from provenance alone
411	- Given I navigate to `/documents`, then I am redirected to `/artifacts` with a
412	  `mediaType=text/markdown` filter pre-applied
413	
414	**E2E Test:** `artifacts.spec.ts` — full workflow: open artifacts list → filter by category → open artifact → verify media renderer → open provenance panel → click Regenerate → verify run link → follow link to run detail → navigate back to artifact → navigate back to list
415	
416	### US-082: Developer Monitors Bead Status on Kanban Board
417	**As a** developer tracking work items
418	**I want** to see beads as cards on a kanban board grouped by status
419	**So that** I can quickly understand what's ready, blocked, and in progress
420	
421	**Acceptance Criteria:**
422	- Given beads exist, when I open the board view, then I see columns for
423	  open, in_progress, and closed with cards sorted by priority
424	- Given beads have dependencies, then blocking/blocked relationships are
425	  visually indicated (dimmed cards, connector lines, or grouping)
426	- Given I drag a card to a new column, then the bead status updates via API
427	  and the card visually moves without a page reload
428	- Given I click a card, then a detail panel opens showing description,
429	  acceptance criteria, execution runs, and the dependency graph
430	- Given I close the detail panel, then I return to the board with my scroll
431	  position and filter state intact
432	
433	**E2E Test:** `beads.spec.ts` — full workflow: open beads board → verify columns and priority sort → drag card to new column → verify status updated → open detail panel → verify all fields → close panel → verify board state preserved
434	
435	### US-082b: Developer Searches and Traverses Beads
436	**As a** developer investigating a work item
437	**I want** to search beads and navigate their dependency graph
438	**So that** I can trace blocking relationships and inspect execution evidence
439	
440	**Acceptance Criteria:**
441	- Given I type in the search bar, then results filter instantly (client-side,
442	  no server round-trip) across title, description, acceptance, and labels
443	- Given I'm viewing a bead's detail, when I click a dependency link, then I
444	  navigate to that bead's detail without a full page reload
445	- Given a bead has linked execution runs, then I see their pass/fail status,
446	  duration, and can expand to see logs
447	- Given I navigate via a dependency link, then the browser Back button returns
448	  me to the originating bead's detail
449	
450	**E2E Test:** `beads.spec.ts` — full workflow: type search term → verify instant filter → open bead detail → click dependency link → verify navigation → expand run log → press Back → verify return to originating bead
451	
452	### US-082c: Developer Views Bead Execution Evidence
453	**As a** developer evaluating whether a bead is truly done
454	**I want** to drill into the execution beads and agent activity associated
455	  with a work item
456	**So that** I can verify tests passed and the implementation matches acceptance
457	
458	**Acceptance Criteria:**
459	- Given a bead has execution runs, when I open its detail, then I see a list
460	  of runs with status, harness, duration, and timestamp
461	- Given I click a run, then I see structured results and raw log output
462	- Given a bead has linked agent activity, then I see runtime summaries and any
463	  available native session references
464	- Given I expand a run and then click back, then the bead detail is still
465	  open showing the same run list
466	
467	**E2E Test:** `beads.spec.ts` — full workflow: open bead with runs → verify run list fields → click run → verify structured results and logs → press Back → verify bead detail still showing run list
468	
469	### US-082d: Supervisor Reviews Bead Against Governing Artifact
470	**As a** supervisor evaluating completed work
471	**I want** to review a bead's implementation against its governing spec and
472	  acceptance criteria side-by-side
473	**So that** I can confirm the work meets the spec or re-open it
474	
475	**Acceptance Criteria:**
476	- Given I click "Review" on a closed bead, then I see the bead's acceptance
477	  criteria alongside the latest execution results and a link to the governing
478	  artifact's content
479	- Given the governing artifact changed since the bead was closed, then a
480	  drift warning badge is shown
481	- Given I determine the work is insufficient, when I click "Re-open" and
482	  provide a reason, then the bead status returns to open with the reason
483	  recorded and the bead appears in the open column immediately
484	- Given I re-open a bead, then the reason is visible in the bead's history
485	
486	**E2E Test:** `beads.spec.ts` — full workflow: open closed bead → click Review → verify AC and results side-by-side → verify drift badge when spec changed → click Re-open with reason → verify bead is open with reason recorded → verify bead appears in open column
487	
488	### US-082e: Supervisor Re-runs Bead Work from Bead Detail
489	**As a** supervisor who wants fresh evidence
490	**I want** to re-run bead work directly from the bead detail
491	**So that** I can verify the implementation still works after spec changes
492	
493	**Acceptance Criteria:**
494	- Given I click "Re-run bead", then the UI dispatches `ddx try <bead>` through
495	  the server worker API; it does not dispatch `ddx run` directly for bead work
496	- Given the re-run is dispatched, then a new worker card appears in the Workers
497	  view and a pending try record appears in the bead's run list within 1s
498	- Given the try completes, then the result appears in the bead's run list with
499	  layer `try`, its child layer-1 `run` records, and the UI refreshes without a
500	  manual reload
501	- Given a bead has a linked execution definition and I click "Run linked
502	  checks", then that definition is dispatched as project checks and displayed
503	  separately from bead re-run history
504	- Given I want to run an agent review, when I click "Agent review", then
505	  an agent session is dispatched with the bead's context as the prompt
506	
507	**E2E Test:** `beads.spec.ts` — full workflow: open bead detail → click Re-run → verify worker appears in Workers view → verify pending try record in run list → wait for completion → verify try + child run records appear → click try record → verify layer-aware detail
508	
509	### US-082f: Developer Navigates from Bead to Related Artifacts
510	**As a** developer exploring a work item
511	**I want** to navigate from a bead to its governing spec, parent bead,
512	  dependencies, and execution evidence in one click
513	**So that** I can build a complete picture without switching to the terminal
514	
515	**Acceptance Criteria:**
516	- Given a bead has `spec-id: FEAT-001`, when I click the spec link, then I
517	  navigate to the document viewer showing FEAT-001's content
518	- Given a bead has a parent, when I click the parent link, then I navigate
519	  to the parent bead's detail
520	- Given a bead has execution runs, when I click a run, then I navigate to
521	  the execution detail view with logs and structured results
522	- Given I followed any of these links, then the browser Back button returns
523	  me to the originating bead's detail at the same scroll position
524	
525	**E2E Test:** `beads.spec.ts` — full workflow: open bead with spec-id and parent → click spec link → verify document viewer → press Back → click parent link → verify parent detail → press Back → click run → verify run detail → press Back → verify return to bead detail
526	
527	### US-083: Developer Edits Document in Browser
528	**As a** developer fixing a stale document
529	**I want** to edit its markdown content in the browser and save
530	**So that** I don't need to switch to my editor for quick fixes
531	
532	**Acceptance Criteria:**
533	- Given I'm viewing a document, when I click "Edit", then I see a markdown editor with the current content
534	- Given I make changes and click "Save", then the file is written to disk via the API
535	- Given the save succeeds, then the rendered view updates and staleness is rechecked
536	- Given I navigate away without saving, then the UI warns me about unsaved changes
537	
538	**E2E Test:** `artifacts.spec.ts` — full workflow: open markdown artifact → click Edit → modify content → click Save → verify rendered view updated → verify staleness rechecked → open artifact again in editor → navigate away → verify unsaved-changes warning
539	
540	### US-084: Developer Searches Across All Documents
541	**As a** developer looking for how something is documented
542	**I want** to search across all document content
543	**So that** I can find relevant docs without knowing their location
544	
545	**Acceptance Criteria:**
546	- Given I type in the search bar, then results appear from all document types
547	- Given results are shown, then I see matching snippets with the search term highlighted
548	- Given I click a result, then I navigate to that document's detail view
549	- Given I press Back, then I return to the search results with my query intact

8	---
9	# Feature: DDx Server Web UI
10	
11	**ID:** FEAT-008
12	**Status:** In Progress
13	**Priority:** P1
14	**Owner:** DDx Team
15	
16	## Overview
17	
18	The DDx server (`ddx-server`) serves a web UI for browsing artifacts, beads,
19	the artifact dependency graph, and DDx run activity. Artifacts include markdown
20	documents plus sidecar-identified media such as diagrams, wireframes, images,
21	and PDFs. The UI is a SvelteKit application built with Svelte 5, embedded into
22	the Go binary via `embed.FS`, and served alongside the MCP and HTTP API
23	endpoints from a single process.
24	
25	`ddx-server` runs as a per-user host daemon (one instance per machine, see
26	FEAT-002 and FEAT-020), so the same UI enumerates every project that the
27	host+user registry knows about and binds its navigation to one selected
28	project context at a time. The node-aware URL structure and combined
29	cross-project dashboards are specified in FEAT-021; FEAT-008 owns the
30	underlying views that those routes render.
31	
32	The UI consumes GraphQL operations defined in `cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql`.
33	Every page lists its required query or subscription by name for traceability.
34	
35	## Problem Statement
36	
37	**Current situation:** DDx artifacts, beads, and the artifact graph are only accessible via CLI commands or MCP tool calls. There's no visual way to browse the artifact library, see the dependency graph, check bead status, regenerate generated artifacts, or review layer-aware run evidence.
38	
39	**Pain points:**
40	- CLI output for `ddx doc graph` is text-only — dependency graphs need visual rendering
41	- Bead status and dependency trees are hard to grok in terminal output
42	- DDx run activity has no browsable interface that preserves the `work` -> `try` -> `run` layers
43	- Non-markdown artifacts have no type-aware browser preview, even when the graph knows their identity
44	- Generated artifacts have no UI affordance for checking provenance or requesting regeneration
45	- New team members can't quickly understand what artifacts exist or how they relate
46	
47	**Desired outcome:** A web dashboard served from the same `ddx-server` binary that already handles MCP and HTTP API. Developers open a browser, see their artifact library, dependency graph, bead board, generated-artifact provenance, and DDx run activity — all from one URL.
48	
49	## Architecture
50	
51	### Single Binary, Multiple Surfaces
52	
53	```
54	ddx-server binary
55	├── /            → Web UI (embedded SvelteKit)
56	├── /api/        → HTTP REST API (JSON) — frozen
57	└── /graphql     → GraphQL endpoint (gqlgen)
58	```
59	
60	The SvelteKit frontend queries the `/graphql` endpoint for all data. REST endpoints
61	remain unchanged and are consumed by the CLI and MCP tools only.
62	
63	### Project Context
64	
65	The UI is rendered under the node-scoped URL structure defined in FEAT-021:
66	`/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/...` for project-scoped views and
67	`/nodes/:nodeId/...` for combined node-wide dashboards. The root `/`
68	redirects to `/nodes/:nodeId` using the ID returned by `GET /api/node`
69	(FEAT-020). When more than one project is registered the navigation bar
70	exposes a project picker populated from `GET /api/projects`; selecting a
71	project swaps the `:projectId` segment in place. All GraphQL queries issued by the
72	UI are bound to the selected project context.
73	
74	### Build Pipeline
75	
76	```
77	cli/internal/server/frontend/   → SvelteKit + TypeScript
78	  bun run build                 → cli/internal/server/frontend/build/
79	cli/internal/server/            → Go with embed.FS
80	  go build                      → ddx-server (single binary with embedded UI)
81	```
82	
83	During development, SvelteKit's dev server proxies `/graphql` to the running Go server.
84	
85	### Technology Choices
86	
87	| Layer | Choice | Why |
88	|-------|--------|-----|
89	| Frontend framework | **Svelte 5** | Compile-time optimizations, no Virtual DOM |
90	| Build tool | **SvelteKit** | Router, layout, API routes, hooks, adapter-static for embedding |
91	| Styling | **Tailwind CSS** | Consistent with website (Hextra uses Tailwind) |
92	| Data fetching | **graphql-request + graphql-ws** | Lightweight GraphQL client with typed queries and WebSocket subscriptions |
93	| Graph visualization | **D3.js** or **Cytoscape.js** | For document dependency graph rendering |
94	| Embedding | **Go embed.FS** | Compile frontend into the binary — no external files needed |
95	| Go HTTP | **Chi** or **net/http** | Standard library compatible routing |
96	| Go GraphQL | **gqlgen** | Schema-first, code-generated resolvers |
97	
98	### Alternatives Considered
99	
100	**Templ + htmx** — simpler (no JS toolchain), but the dependency graph visualization and interactive bead board need more interactivity than htmx provides well. Templ + htmx is great for CRUD; we need a richer graph/dashboard experience.
101	
102	**Local-first with CRDT sync** — interesting for offline use but overkill for v1. The UI is primarily read-heavy (browsing docs, viewing graphs). Can be added later with PowerSync or ElectricSQL if needed.
103	
104	## Requirements
105	
106	### Functional
107	
108	1. **Artifact browser, viewer, editor, and regeneration**
109	   - List artifacts by category (prompts, personas, patterns, templates, ADRs,
110	     SDs, diagrams, wireframes, generated images, PDFs)
111	   - Full-text search across textual artifact content, sidecar metadata, and
112	     markdown frontmatter
113	   - View markdown artifacts rendered as markdown with syntax highlighting
114	   - Render artifacts by `media_type`:
115	     - `text/markdown`: markdown renderer with syntax-highlighted code blocks
116	     - `image/svg+xml` with Mermaid provenance: sanitized inline SVG preview
117	     - `application/vnd.excalidraw+json`: Excalidraw read-only embed
118	     - `image/*`: inline image preview with metadata fallback
119	     - `application/pdf`: embedded PDF preview with open-file fallback
120	     - unknown or binary media: metadata panel plus open/download affordance
121	   - Show `ddx:` frontmatter or sidecar `.ddx.yaml` metadata (id,
122	     `media_type`, dependencies, `generated_by`, and staleness status)
123	   - Clickable dependency links — navigate upstream/downstream docs inline
124	   - **Editor**: in-browser markdown editing with save-to-disk (calls API to write file)
125	   - Sidebar showing document dependency tree for current doc
126	   - Staleness indicator per document (fresh/stale/missing-deps)
127	   - Generated-artifact provenance indicator: show producing run id, prompt or
128	     generator summary, and source-hash status when `generated_by` is present
129	   - Generated-artifact staleness is artifact-local: a `generated_by.source_hash`
130	     mismatch marks only the generated artifact stale and does not cascade like
131	     `depends_on`
132	   - Regenerate action for generated artifacts calls the `artifactRegenerate`
133	     mutation, returns the new run id, and links directly to the produced run
134	     detail and updated artifact
135	
136	2. **Document dependency graph**
137	   - Interactive visual graph (D3/Cytoscape) showing all document relationships
138	   - Color-code nodes by staleness (green=fresh, orange=stale, red=missing-deps)
139	   - Click node to navigate to document detail
140	   - Filter by document type, staleness status
141	   - Zoom/pan for large graphs
142	   - Layout options (hierarchical top-down, force-directed)
143	
144	3. **Bead views**
145	
146	   The beads UI runs entirely in the browser. On load, the client fetches the
147	   full bead set from the `beads` query (GraphQL). All search, filter, sort, and graph
148	   traversal happens client-side using an in-browser data layer.
149	
150	   **Master-detail list view:**
151	   - Searchable list: full-text across title, description, acceptance, labels
152	   - Sortable columns: ID, title, status, priority, labels, owner, updated_at
153	   - Filterable by status, priority, label, owner
154	   - Click a bead to open detail panel (split pane or slide-over)
155	   - Detail shows: all fields, dependency tree, execution beads, agent sessions
156	   - Graph traversal: click a dependency → navigate to that bead's detail
157	   - Drill-down into run records linked to the bead (shows layer, pass/fail,
158	     logs, duration)
159	
160	   **Kanban board view:**
161	   - Columns represent status: `open`, `in_progress`, `closed`
162	   - Cards within columns ordered by priority (P0 top, P4 bottom)
163	   - Dependency grouping: beads that block each other cluster visually
164	     (similar to Trello, Fuzz by 37signals, GitHub Projects)
165	   - Drag-and-drop to change status (calls `beadUpdate` mutation)
166	   - Swimlanes option: group rows by label (e.g., `area:cli`, `area:web`)
167	   - Color coding: priority → card border, labels → chips, blocked → dimmed
168	   - Collapsed card shows: title, priority badge, label chips, dep count
169	   - Expanded card shows: description, acceptance, execution summary
170	
171	   **Ready queue view:**
172	   - Filtered view showing only beads with all dependencies satisfied
173	   - Sorted by priority, then by creation date
174	   - One-click claim (calls `beadClaim` mutation)
175	   - Single-ticket ready work is shown ahead of epics; open epics appear in a
176	     separate epic lane rather than the ordinary execute-loop queue
177	
178	   **Bead detail actions:**
179	
180	   The detail panel is not just a viewer — it is the primary interaction
181	   surface for evaluating and driving work items.

183	   *Navigation:*
184	   - `spec-id` links to the governing artifact (rendered inline or navigates
185	     to the document viewer with that artifact's content)
186	   - Parent bead link navigates to the parent's detail
187	   - Dependency links navigate to each dep's detail
188	   - Run records link to the layer-aware run detail view (logs, results)

193	     vs the latest execution results and implementation diff. The user or
194	     agent evaluates whether the work actually meets the spec.
195	   - "Re-review" action on closed beads: re-evaluates against current
196	     artifact state — did the governing spec change since the bead was
197	     closed? If so, flag as potentially stale.
198	   - Artifact drift indicator: if the `spec-id` artifact's content hash
199	     changed since the bead was last updated, show a warning badge.

1074	//go:embed all:frontend/build
1075	var frontendFiles embed.FS
1076	
1077	func main() {
1078	    distFS, _ := fs.Sub(frontendFiles, "frontend/build") // relative to embed.go in cli/internal/server/
1079	    
1080	    mux := http.NewServeMux()
1081	    mux.Handle("/api/", apiRouter)

1086	// spaHandler serves static files, falling back to index.html for SPA routing
1087	func spaHandler(fs http.FileSystem) http.Handler {
1088	    fileServer := http.FileServer(fs)
1089	    return http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
1090	        if _, err := fs.Open(r.URL.Path); err != nil {
1091	            r.URL.Path = "/"
1092	        }
1093	        fileServer.ServeHTTP(w, r)
1094	    })
1095	}

9	# Feature: Three-Layer Run Architecture
10	
11	**ID:** FEAT-010
12	**Status:** In Progress
13	**Priority:** P1
14	**Owner:** DDx Team
15	
16	## Overview
17	
18	DDx owns three explicit, layered run primitives. Each higher layer composes
19	the layer beneath it. There are no other run kinds beyond these three.
20	
21	| Layer | CLI | Inputs | Outputs / side effects | Owns |
22	|---|---|---|---|---|
23	| 1 | `ddx run` | prompt + agent config | structured output, side effects, run metadata (tokens, model, duration, exit) | invocation atom; consumes upstream `ddx-agent` per CONTRACT-003 |
24	| 2 | `ddx try <bead>` | bead id, base revision | new worktree state + agent-run evidence; merge or preserve | worktree start/end capture; bead → prompt resolution; side-effect bundling |
25	| 3 | `ddx work` | bead queue, stop conditions | sequence of `ddx try` records + loop-level record (drained / blocked / deferred) | mechanical queue drain; no-progress detection |
26	
27	`ddx try` wraps `ddx run`. `ddx work` iterates `ddx try`. One on-disk
28	substrate; layer metadata distinguishes records.
29	
30	`ddx artifact regenerate <id>` is sugar over layer 1 (or layer 2 when the
31	generator edits the repo) with `produces_artifact: <id>` metadata. It is
32	not a fourth layer — it is a labeled invocation at an existing layer.
33	
34	## Problem Statement
35	
36	**Current situation:** DDx has accreted two independent run-storage shapes
37	— `.ddx/exec-runs/` for generic execution evidence and
38	`.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/` for tracked bead-attempt bundles. Loop behavior,
39	worktree lifecycle, and bead resolution are spread across legacy command
40	surfaces without a clean layering story. There is no single place to introspect "what runs
41	happened, at what layer, with what evidence."
42	
43	**Pain points:**
44	- Two on-disk record shapes for fundamentally similar artifacts (a single
45	  agent invocation, with logs, structured result, and provenance)
46	- No first-class concept of the three composing layers (atom / attempt /
47	  drain), so consumers must intuit which surface to read
48	- Stop conditions for queue drain are implicit and not observable
49	- Generated-artifact provenance has no clean hook into the run record
50	- Open-ended catalog pressure: every new "run flavor" (benchmark, replay,
51	  comparison) tempts a new on-disk shape or a new top-level verb
52	
53	**Desired outcome:** A single on-disk run substrate with explicit layer
54	metadata, three top-level CLI verbs that map 1:1 onto the layers, and a
55	narrow read-only HTTP/MCP write surface limited to artifact regeneration.
56	
57	## Three-Layer Architecture
58	
59	### Layer 1 — `ddx run` (invocation atom)
60	
61	A layer-1 run is one agent invocation. Inputs are a prompt, requested
62	`MinPower` and optional `MaxPower`, optional agent passthrough constraints, and
63	non-routing execution config;
64	outputs are the structured response (text or bytes), any side-effects the agent
65	performed via tools, and run metadata (tokens, model, actual power, duration,
66	exit status, session pointer).
67	
68	Layer 1 calls the upstream `ddx-agent` service contract directly. DDx does not
69	reimplement agent routing or the invocation loop; it wraps one `Execute` call
70	with provenance capture.
71	
72	`ddx artifact regenerate <id>` is layer 1 when the generator returns
73	bytes/text and DDx writes the file; it is layer 2 when the generator
74	edits the repo directly (bead-less worktree).
75	
76	### Layer 2 — `ddx try <bead>` (bead attempt)
77	
78	A layer-2 run is one bead attempt in an isolated worktree. It owns:
79	
80	- Worktree creation from a base revision and worktree finalization
81	  (merge or preserve)
82	- Bead → prompt resolution (description, acceptance, governing artifacts)
83	- Side-effect bundling (commits, evidence, no-changes rationale)
84	- One or more layer-1 invocations recorded inside the attempt record
85	
86	A layer-2 record references its child layer-1 records by run id.
87	
88	Layer 2 owns bead-attempt success classification. DDx determines success from
89	the artifacts it owns: commit presence, merge/preserve result, no-changes
90	rationale, post-run checks, review verdicts, and cooldown policy. The agent's
91	exit status and actual model/power are inputs to that decision, not the whole
92	decision.
93	
94	### Layer 3 — `ddx work` (queue drain)
95	
96	A layer-3 run is one drain of the bead queue. It iterates `ddx try`
97	across ready beads until a stop condition is met. It owns:
98	
99	- Queue iteration order
100	- No-progress / stop-condition evaluation
101	- A loop-level record that references its child layer-2 records by
102	  attempt id and reports terminal disposition (drained, blocked,
103	  deferred, no-progress)
104	
105	Content-aware supervisory decisions (e.g., "comparison failed → enqueue
106	reconciliation beads") are not layer-3 concerns. Those are skill or
107	plugin compositions on top of the three layers.
108	
109	## Agent Power and Retry
110	
111	DDx owns retry policy between bead attempts. If an attempt produces classified
112	evidence that more capable reasoning could plausibly change the result, DDx may
113	retry the bead with a higher `MinPower`.
114	
115	The agent owns routing within the requested power bounds. A retry request says
116	"use at least this much power" by raising `MinPower`; an explicit `MaxPower`, if
117	supplied, remains a hard upper bound. Operator-supplied `--harness`,
118	`--provider`, and `--model` values may be sent to the agent as passthrough
119	constraints, but DDx does not interpret them. Each layer-1 record stores:
120

160	- `blocked_by_passthrough_constraint` / `agent_power_unsatisfied`
161	- any status where retry would require DDx to inspect, remove, rewrite, or
162	  substitute `--harness`, `--provider`, or `--model`
163	
164	Every retry/stop decision records the classification and evidence used. The
165	policy may inspect DDx attempt outcomes and the agent's typed status; it must
166	not branch on concrete provider/model identity.
167	
168	## Substrate Unification
169	
170	### One record shape
171	
172	All three layers persist to a single on-disk shape under
173	`.ddx/runs/<run-id>/`. The record has the following common fields and a
174	discriminating `layer` field; layer-specific extensions live in named
175	subobjects rather than separate file trees.
176	
177	Common fields:
178	- `run_id` — stable, sortable identifier
179	- `layer` — `1` | `2` | `3`
180	- `parent_run_id` — pointer to the enclosing layer's run, if any
181	- `started_at` / `finished_at`
182	- `terminal_status` — `success` | `failure` | `timeout` | `error` |
183	  `no_progress` (layer 3 only) | `preserved` (layer 2 only)
184	- `actor`, `host`, `git_revision`, `ddx_version`
185	- `produces_artifact` — optional artifact id when this run regenerates
186	  an artifact
187	- `attachments[]` — references to large bodies (logs, structured payloads,
188	  bundles) stored alongside the record rather than inline
189	
190	Layer extensions:
191	- `layer1` — prompt reference, agent config, model id, token usage,
192	  upstream session id, structured response pointer
193	- `layer2` — bead id, base revision, worktree path, finalization mode
194	  (`merge` | `preserve`), child layer-1 run ids, evidence-bundle pointer
195	- `layer3` — queue snapshot pointer, stop-condition evaluation log,
196	  child layer-2 attempt ids, drain disposition
197	
198	Records are append-only. A new run never rewrites a previous one.
199	
200	### On-disk migration
201	
202	The two pre-existing trees collapse into the unified substrate:
203	
204	- `.ddx/exec-runs/<run-id>/` → `.ddx/runs/<run-id>/` with `layer: 1`
205	- `.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/` → `.ddx/runs/<run-id>/` with `layer: 2`
206	  and the existing prompt / manifest / result / checks / provenance
207	  surfaced as layer-2 evidence-bundle attachments
208	
209	Layer-3 records are new (no pre-existing tree).
210	
211	Migration is one-way and read-compatible:
212	- Writers always target `.ddx/runs/`
213	- Readers tolerate legacy paths during a documented migration window
214	  and surface them through the unified read API as if they were layer-1
215	  or layer-2 records, then a one-shot rewrite normalizes them on disk
216	- After the migration window closes, legacy paths are removed; readers
217	  no longer fall back
218	
219	The migration window, the rewrite tool, and the cutoff signal are
220	specified in the implementation epic, not here.
221	
222	## `ddx work` Stop Conditions
223	
224	`ddx work` drains the queue until **any** of the following stop
225	conditions is met. The terminal disposition of the layer-3 record names
226	which condition fired.
227	
228	1. **`drained`** — queue is empty (no ready beads remain).
229	2. **`blocked`** — every remaining ready bead has produced a terminal
230	   non-success outcome on its most recent attempt and re-attempt is not
231	   permitted under the current policy.
232	3. **`deferred`** — a configured wall-clock or attempt-count budget is
233	   exhausted. The remaining queue is intact and a subsequent `ddx work`
234	   resumes from where this one stopped.
235	4. **`no_progress`** — `N` consecutive `ddx try` attempts produced no
236	   commit and no merged side-effect on the base branch (default
237	   `N = 3`; configurable). This is the mechanical "we are spinning"
238	   detector, not a content-aware judgment.
239	5. **`signal`** — a SIGINT/SIGTERM was received between attempts. The
240	   in-flight `ddx try` (if any) finalizes per its own merge/preserve
241	   policy before the loop record closes.
242	
243	Stop-condition evaluation runs **between** `ddx try` invocations; an
244	in-flight attempt is never aborted to satisfy a stop condition (`signal`
245	excepted, and only after the in-flight attempt finalizes).
246	
247	### Long-running default (`--poll-interval`)
248	
249	Per ddx-dc157075, the default `--poll-interval` for both `ddx work` and
250	`ddx agent execute-loop` is **30s**. With a positive poll interval the
251	worker stays alive across empty polls (`drained` becomes a transient
252	"running (idle)" substate, not a terminal exit). The loop exits only on
253	the conditions enumerated above plus `signal`, fatal config errors, or
254	the explicit operator opt-outs:
255	
256	- `--once` — process at most one ready bead, then exit.
257	- `--poll-interval=0` — legacy "drain-and-exit" semantics: when the
258	  queue empties, return immediately without polling.
259	
260	Server-managed workers spawned via `POST /api/agent/workers/execute-loop`
261	inherit the same 30s default when the request omits `poll_interval`. The
262	worker record exposes a `substate` field set to `"idle"` while the loop
263	is sleeping between empty polls; it is cleared as soon as a candidate is
264	picked. Terminal worker states (`exited`, `failed`, `stopped`, `reaped`)
265	always clear `substate`.
266	
267	The structured `loop.end` event carries an `exit_reason` field
268	(`once_complete`, `explicit_poll_zero`, `sigint`, `sigterm`,
269	`fatal_config`; `providers_exhausted` is reserved for the quota-pause
270	work in ddx-aede917d).
271	
272	Power escalation is evaluated between attempts as part of retry policy. A
273	higher-power retry raises `MinPower` and resets neither the evidence history nor
274	the no-progress counter; it is recorded as the next `ddx try` with its own
275	requested bounds and actual power metadata.
276	
277	The evaluation log is persisted on the layer-3 record so a human or
278	tool can audit which condition fired and on which iteration.
279	
280	## No Run-Type Catalog
281	
282	DDx will not introduce additional run kinds beyond the three layers.
283	Comparison, replay, benchmark, adversarial review, effort estimate,
284	bead breakdown, and similar workflows are **skill compositions** over
285	the three layers — they emit ordinary layer-1, layer-2, or layer-3
286	records, optionally tagged with a free-form `skill` label, and do not
287	get bespoke storage shapes, bespoke verbs, or bespoke spec sections.
288	
289	This is a load-bearing non-goal: every future "we should add a fourth
290	layer for X" proposal is rejected by reference to this section. If a
291	new workflow cannot be expressed as a composition over `run` / `try` /
292	`work`, the gap is in the three layers' contract, not in the catalog.
293	
294	## Requirements
295	
296	### Functional
297	
298	1. **One on-disk substrate** — all run records, regardless of layer,
299	   persist under `.ddx/runs/<run-id>/` with the common record shape and
300	   a discriminating `layer` field.
301	2. **Layer metadata** — every run record carries `layer`,
302	   `parent_run_id`, and the layer-appropriate extension subobject.
303	3. **Layer-1 invocation** — `ddx run` produces exactly one layer-1
304	   record per invocation; consumes upstream `ddx-agent` per
305	   CONTRACT-003.
306	4. **Layer-2 attempt** — `ddx try <bead>` produces exactly one layer-2
307	   record, references its child layer-1 records, and finalizes the
308	   worktree as `merge` or `preserve`.
309	5. **Layer-3 drain** — `ddx work` produces exactly one layer-3 record
310	   per invocation, references its child layer-2 attempt ids, and reports
311	   one of the five terminal dispositions (`drained`, `blocked`,
312	   `deferred`, `no_progress`, `signal`).
313	6. **Stop conditions** — `ddx work` evaluates the five stop conditions
314	   between attempts and persists the evaluation log on the layer-3
315	   record.
316	7. **No-progress detection** — the `no_progress` condition is mechanical
317	   (no commit, no merged side-effect) and configurable via
318	   `.ddx/config.yaml` (default `N = 3`).
319	8. **Append-only history** — a new run never rewrites a previous record
320	   at any layer.
321	9. **Cross-layer query** — `ddx runs` lists records across all three
322	   layers with layer filtering; `ddx tries` and `ddx work workers`
323	   are layer-specific projections over the same substrate.
324	10. **Run detail inspection** — for any run id, callers can retrieve the
325	    common fields, the layer extension, attachments, and child run ids.
326	11. **Generated-artifact provenance** — runs that regenerate an artifact
327	    set `produces_artifact: <id>` on the run record; FEAT-007 reads this
328	    to populate `generated_by` graph edges.
329	12. **Migration from legacy paths** — readers tolerate
330	    `.ddx/exec-runs/` and `.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/` during a
331	    documented migration window and surface them as layer-1 / layer-2
332	    records; writers always target `.ddx/runs/`.
333	13. **Configuration** — substrate root, no-progress threshold, deferred
334	    budget, and retention settings are configurable in
335	    `.ddx/config.yaml`.
336	
337	### Non-Functional
338	
339	- **Determinism:** the persisted record reflects the exact prompt,
340	  response, logs, and structured result of one invocation.
341	- **Durability:** record writes are atomic or serialized so concurrent
342	  writers cannot leave partial records.
343	- **Observability:** stored records are human-readable enough for
344	  debugging and machine-parseable enough for automation.
345	- **Portability:** records remain repo-local and file-backed; no hosted
346	  service or database is required.
347	- **Low overhead:** layer-1 record bookkeeping adds minimal overhead
348	  beyond the underlying agent invocation.
349	- **Payload resilience:** large prompt, response, log, and bundle
350	  bodies are stored as attachments and never require rewriting a shared
351	  history file to persist one run safely.
352	
353	## CLI Commands
354	
355	The three top-level verbs map 1:1 onto the layers:
356	
357	```bash
358	ddx run --prompt <file> [--agent <name>]                # layer 1
359	ddx try <bead-id> [--from <rev>] [--no-merge]           # layer 2

350	  bodies are stored as attachments and never require rewriting a shared
351	  history file to persist one run safely.
352	
353	## CLI Commands
354	
355	The three top-level verbs map 1:1 onto the layers:
356	
357	```bash
358	ddx run --prompt <file> [--agent <name>]                # layer 1
359	ddx try <bead-id> [--from <rev>] [--no-merge]           # layer 2
360	ddx work [--workers N] [--budget <duration>]            # layer 3
361	```
362	
363	Cross-layer evidence introspection lives under `ddx runs`:
364	
365	```bash
366	ddx runs list [--layer 1|2|3] [--artifact ID] [--bead ID]
367	ddx runs show <run-id>                                  # full record + layer extension
368	ddx runs log <run-id>                                   # raw logs / attachments
369	ddx runs result <run-id> [--json]                       # structured result
370	```
371	
372	Layer-specific projections:
373	
374	```bash
375	ddx tries list [--bead ID]                              # layer-2 only
376	ddx tries show <attempt-id>
377	ddx work workers list                                   # layer-3 worker mgmt
378	```
379	
380	`ddx artifact regenerate <id>` is sugar over `ddx run` (or `ddx try`
381	when the generator edits the repo) with `produces_artifact: <id>`
382	recorded on the run.
383	
384	## Server Endpoints (FEAT-002 integration)
385	
386	| MCP Tool | HTTP Endpoint | Description | Mode |
387	|----------|--------------|-------------|------|
388	| `ddx_runs_list` | `GET /api/runs` | List run records across layers, filterable by `layer`, `artifact`, `bead` | read |
389	| `ddx_runs_show` | `GET /api/runs/:id` | Show one run record with layer extension and attachment refs | read |
390	| `ddx_runs_log` | `GET /api/runs/:id/log` | Raw logs / attachment bodies | read |
391	| `ddx_runs_result` | `GET /api/runs/:id/result` | Structured result payload | read |
392	| `ddx_artifact_regenerate` | `POST /api/artifacts/:id/regenerate` | Trigger regeneration of one artifact; returns the new run id | **write — narrow** |
393	
394	The HTTP/MCP read surface is total over the unified run substrate.
395	
396	The **only** write surface added by this feature is
397	`artifactRegenerate`. All other run invocation (layer-1 ad-hoc, layer-2
398	bead attempts, layer-3 queue drain) remains CLI-only. Additional write
399	endpoints require a separate feature update; they are not implicit in
400	this read-coverage expansion.
401	
402	## User Stories
403	
404	### US-090: Developer Inspects a Layer-1 Run
405	
406	**As a** developer running an ad-hoc agent invocation
407	**I want** to inspect the resulting run record
408	**So that** I can audit prompt, response, tokens, and side-effects
409	
410	**Acceptance Criteria:**
411	- Given I invoke `ddx run`, then DDx writes one layer-1 record under
412	  `.ddx/runs/<run-id>/` with `layer: 1` and the layer-1 extension
413	  populated.
414	- Given the run completes, when I run `ddx runs show <run-id>`, then I
415	  see common fields, the layer-1 extension, requested `MinPower`/`MaxPower`,
416	  actual model/power, requested passthrough constraints if supplied, and attachment
417	  references.
418	
419	### US-091: Developer Inspects a Layer-2 Bead Attempt
420	
421	**As a** developer reviewing a bead attempt
422	**I want** to see the attempt's worktree finalization and the child
423	layer-1 invocations
424	**So that** I can audit what the agent did inside the attempt
425	
426	**Acceptance Criteria:**
427	- Given I invoke `ddx try <bead-id>`, then DDx writes one layer-2
428	  record with `layer: 2` referencing one or more layer-1 run ids.
429	- Given the attempt finalizes, when I run `ddx runs show <run-id>`,
430	  then the record reports `merge` or `preserve` and surfaces the
431	  evidence bundle.
432	
433	### US-092: Developer Drains the Queue and Audits the Drain
434	
435	**As a** developer running `ddx work`
436	**I want** the loop to stop on a clearly named condition
437	**So that** I can tell whether the queue was drained, blocked, deferred,
438	spinning, or interrupted
439	
440	**Acceptance Criteria:**
441	- Given I invoke `ddx work`, then DDx writes exactly one layer-3 record
442	  per invocation referencing each child layer-2 attempt id.
443	- Given the loop stops, then the layer-3 record's terminal disposition
444	  is one of `drained`, `blocked`, `deferred`, `no_progress`, `signal`,
445	  and the evaluation log names which condition fired and on which
446	  iteration.
447	
448	### US-093: No-Progress Detection Stops a Spinning Loop
449	
450	**As a** developer running `ddx work`
451	**I want** the loop to stop when consecutive attempts make no progress
452	**So that** I do not burn budget on a pathological queue state
453	
454	**Acceptance Criteria:**
455	- Given `N` consecutive `ddx try` attempts produce no commit and no
456	  merged side-effect (default `N = 3`, configurable), then the next
457	  stop-condition evaluation triggers `no_progress` and the layer-3
458	  record terminates with that disposition.
459	- Given retry policy permits escalation before `no_progress` fires, when
460	  a retry is scheduled, then the next layer-1 run raises `MinPower`
461	  and records the actual model/power returned by the agent.
462	- Given the original invocation supplied `--harness`, `--provider`, or
463	  `--model`, then retries pass the same values through unchanged and DDx does
464	  not inspect those values when choosing the next requested `MinPower`.
465	- Given retry escalation would exceed the power available under hard
466	  passthrough pins, then DDx stops with `blocked_by_passthrough_constraint` or
467	  `agent_power_unsatisfied`, records the agent-supplied evidence, and does not
468	  mutate the pins or call `ResolveRoute`.
469	
470	### US-094: Cross-Layer Run History
471	
472	**As a** workflow tool
473	**I want** to query run history across all three layers from one place
474	**So that** I do not have to merge two on-disk shapes by hand
475	
476	**Acceptance Criteria:**
477	- Given runs exist at all three layers, when a tool calls
478	  `GET /api/runs?layer=...`, then it receives records of the requested
479	  layer with consistent common fields.
480	- Given a layer-3 record exists, then its child layer-2 attempt ids and
481	  their child layer-1 run ids are reachable by following
482	  `parent_run_id` pointers.
483	
484	### US-095: Artifact Regeneration Records Provenance
485	
486	**As a** consumer of the document graph
487	**I want** generated artifacts to point back to the run that produced
488	them
489	**So that** staleness can be evaluated against generator inputs
490	
491	**Acceptance Criteria:**
492	- Given I regenerate an artifact via `ddx artifact regenerate <id>`,
493	  then the resulting run record has `produces_artifact: <id>`.
494	- Given a generated artifact has been produced by a run, then
495	  FEAT-007's `generated_by` edge resolves to that run id.
496	
497	### US-096: Operator Migrates From Legacy Run Trees
498	
499	**As a** repo operator adopting the unified substrate
500	**I want** legacy `.ddx/exec-runs/` and `.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/`
501	records to remain inspectable during the migration window
502	**So that** adopting `.ddx/runs/` does not strand prior evidence
503	
504	**Acceptance Criteria:**
505	- Given a repository contains legacy run data, when DDx reads run
506	  history, then legacy paths surface through the unified read API as
507	  layer-1 / layer-2 records during the documented migration window.
508	- Given the migration window closes, then writers target only
509	  `.ddx/runs/` and a one-shot rewrite has normalized prior records into
510	  the unified substrate.
511	
512	## CONTRACT-003 Amendment
513	
514	This feature requires exactly one read-only-amendment-adjacent change
515	to CONTRACT-003: a narrow write surface for `artifactRegenerate` on the
516	HTTP/MCP layer. No other write surfaces are added; in particular,
517	layer-1, layer-2, and layer-3 invocation remain CLI-only.
518	
519	If FEAT-006 implementation reveals additional CONTRACT-003 gaps (e.g.,
520	upstream Cobra root export details), those amendments are scoped to
521	FEAT-006 and not to this feature.
522	
523	## Dependencies
524	
525	- FEAT-005 (Artifacts) — `produces_artifact` references artifact IDs
526	- FEAT-006 (Agent Service) — provides the layer-1 consumer-side wrapper
527	  and CONTRACT-003 boundary
528	- FEAT-007 (Doc Graph) — `generated_by` edges read `produces_artifact`
529	  from run records
530	- DDx CLI infrastructure (config loading, command factory)
531	
532	## Out of Scope
533	
534	- **Run kinds beyond the three layers** — comparison, replay, benchmark,
535	  adversarial review, effort estimate, and similar workflows are skill
536	  compositions, not new layers or new on-disk shapes.
537	- **Content-aware supervisory loop decisions** — `ddx work` performs
538	  mechanical queue drain only. Decisions like "comparison failed →
539	  enqueue reconciliation beads" are skill / plugin / workflow-tool
540	  territory.
541	- **Server-side layer-1/2/3 invocation** — only `artifactRegenerate`
542	  has a write endpoint in this feature.
543	- **Workflow-specific action semantics** — phase routing, methodology
544	  scoring, tool-specific issue closing — delegated to workflow tools.
545	- **Autonomy semantics and escalation policy** — DDx provides the
546	  substrate; workflow tools decide policy.
547	- **Hosted run history storage** — records are repo-local and
548	  file-backed.
549	- **Separate domain-specific runtime stores** — there is one substrate.
